title: NOC 41409 – Other Professional Occupations in Social Science | GTR Immigration
description: Other Professional Occupations in Social Science under NOC 41409 include highly specialized researchers and academics such as anthropologists, archaeologists, sociologists, political scientists, criminologists, linguists, historians, geographers, and psychometricians. Classified under TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), these roles contribute to research, policy development, education, and institutional analysis across Canada. These professionals work in universities, research institutions, government agencies, think tanks, consulting firms, museums, and public policy organizations.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41409

duties: Study the origin and development of human societies and cultures
Analyze human evolution and geographical distribution
Reconstruct past economic and social life
Study crime patterns, prevention, and rehabilitation
Study relationships between physical and social systems
Study language origin, structure, and development
Apply linguistic theory to translation and communication
Study social structures and societal behavior
Conduct demographic and social trend research
employment_requirements: Master’s or doctoral degree in the relevant discipline is usually required
In Quebec, criminologists must register with the regulatory body to use the protected title
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Professionals under NOC 41409 typically earn between:
$36,600 – $113,600 per year,depending on specialization, sector, and academic level.
Senior researchers and university professors may earn significantly higher compensation.
